title: Helictotrichon austroibericum
taxonomy: Helictotrichon austroibericum is classified within the family Poaceae, which includes various types of grasses. It belongs to the genus Helictotrichon, a group of ornamental grasses known for their feathery plumes and drought tolerance. The species austroibericum is native to South America, where it grows in grasslands and rocky areas. Helictotrichon austroibericum is a perennial grass that produces bright green leaves and delicate white flowers in the summer months. Within the Poaceae family, Helictotrichon austroibericum is further classified as a member of the subfamily Pooideae.

intro: Helictotrichon austroibericum, also known as Iberian blue oat grass, is a perennial grass species within the Poaceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to improve respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments.
